insight.headline: Storefront API Update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive can be used to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel, such as online store or point of sale.
insight.developer_impact: Developers using the Storefront API should update their queries to include the channelId argument when necessary. This change enables more flexibility in querying data specific to different sales channels. Developers should review their existing queries and update them to take advantage of this new feature. The Storefront API documentation should be consulted for more information on using the @inContext directive with channelId.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants may see more accurate and relevant data in their sales channels, such as online store or point of sale, as a result of this update.
insight.why_it_matters: This update provides more flexibility and accuracy in querying data specific to different sales channels, which can improve the overall merchant experience.

also_worth_knowing.text: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials
Full-stack capabilities to power app analytics

updates.title: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials
updates.summary: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials, making it simpler to build theme pages.

updates.title: Full-stack capabilities to power app analytics
updates.summary: Full-stack capabilities are now available to power app analytics, making it easier for app developers to build merchant-facing analytics.

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is now available, giving developers early access to new inventory APIs in the GraphQL Admin API.
The CLI has been updated to version 4.5.2, fixing a warning when running theme commands in valid theme directories.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: Hydrogen has been updated to version 13.0.2, adding non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags to hydrogen link.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: Updated App Store requirements include using a unique name for your app to reduce merchant confusion.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API now supports the channelId argument in the @inContext directive, allowing for channel-specific context in queries.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: New fields have been added to the ExchangeLineItem type, including productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions now include discount allocations to bundle components, allowing apps to access component-level discount allocations.
The CLI has been updated to version 4.5.1, bumping cli-hydrogen to 13.0.1.
The CLI has been updated to version 4.5.0, introducing new store commands, including listing and store creation.

recap.headline: Shopify updates Liquid, GraphQL Admin API, and Storefront API with new features and requirements

themes.title: Liquid and Theme Updates
themes.detail: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials, and theme commands have been fixed in the latest CLI version 4.5.2. Additionally, the physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API.

themes.title: API and Security Updates
themes.detail: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, and the Storefront API `@inContext` directive supports `channelId`. The `ExchangeLineItem` type has also been updated with new fields.

themes.title: App Development and Requirements
themes.detail: App developers must use a unique name for their app, as per the updated App Store requirements. The Hydrogen CLI has been updated to version 13.0.2 with new flags and options.

actions.text: Update apps to use the new Liquid template features
Implement mTLS client certificate for card deposit endpoint
Review and update app names to comply with App Store requirements
